trivia_bullets.text: The Eldest Reborn was originally printed in Dominaria (DOM, no. 90, 2018), an Uncommon Saga enchantment. In this corpus it appears as a Mystery Booster / The List insert (PLST code DOM-90). The card's name references Nicol Bolas — canonically the eldest of Magic's original Elder Dragon cycle and the primary antagonist across the Dominaria–War of the Spark arc.
sources: Draftsim: All 32 Elder Dragons Ranked via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: The three saga chapters mechanically echo Bolas's narrative arc in Dominaria: Chapter I (opponent sacrifices) = Bolas destroying or corrupting allies; Chapter II (opponent discards) = psychic assault; Chapter III (put a creature or planeswalker from a graveyard onto the battlefield) = the actual rebirth. The chapter structure as lore storyboarding is a core Dominaria Saga design principle.
sources: MTG Assist: The Eldest Reborn card page via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: In Nicol Bolas, the Ravager Commander builds, The Eldest Reborn appears in approximately 52% of decks (~8,990 total) with 50% synergy score; in Bolas Planeswalker-focused builds, 63% of decks. One of the highest rates for any non-marquee Dominaria uncommon — driven almost entirely by the flavor of including a 'Bolas is reborn' card in a Bolas commander deck.
sources: EDHREC: Nicol Bolas the Ravager commander page via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Ravenna Tran (also credited as Jenn Ravenna Tran / Jenn Ravenna). This card is one of the anchor prints for the cards/_artists/jenn-ravenna-tran.md artist node in the corpus. The composition — skeletal figure rising from a pit in murky greens and sickly yellows — is representative of Tran's dark-fantasy range.
No flavor text on this printing; the three chapter abilities fill the text box entirely.

related_cards.name: Nicol Bolas, the Ravager
related_cards.note: the card this Saga is named for; top EDHREC commander that includes this card at 52%

related_cards.name: Nicol Bolas, the Deceiver
